---
title: "GSE22862"
output: pdf_document
date: "2025-03-26"
---

Upload libraries:

```{r}
library("GEOquery")
library(ggplot2)
library(useful) 
```

Get data:

```{r}
gse <- getGEO("GSE22862", destdir = ".", getGPL = FALSE)
gse <- gse[[1]]
#gse<- getGEO(file = "GSE22862_series_matrix.txt.gz", getGPL = FALSE)
```
Metadata extraction:
```{r}
metadata <- pData(gse)
metadata$label <- ifelse(metadata$source_name_ch1 == "primary lung cancer", "T",
                         ifelse(metadata$source_name_ch1 == "Normal lung", "N", NA))
saveRDS(metadata, file = "metadata.rds")
```
Inspect matrix of expression values and plot the bloxplot:

```{r}
ex <- exprs(gse)
saveRDS(ex, file = "expression_matrix_GSE22862.rds")
dim(ex)
colnames(ex)
par(mar=c(6,2,2,1))
# Example: match labels to columns of ex
labels <- metadata$label[match(colnames(ex), metadata$geo_accession)]
boxplot(ex, las=3, names = labels)
```

PCA: 

```{r}
pca <- prcomp(t(ex))
screeplot(pca)
```

Plot of PCA:

```{r}
# Extract sample names from PCA
sample_names <- rownames(pca$x)

# Extract group info from metadata using sample names
group <- metadata[sample_names, "label"]

# Assign colors based on group labels
grpcol <- ifelse(group == "T", "red", "blue")

# PCA plots
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2], xlab="PCA1", ylab="PCA2", 
     main="PCA: Components 1 & 2", col=grpcol, pch=16)
legend("topright", legend=c("T", "N"), col=c("red", "blue"), pch=16)

plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,3], xlab="PCA1", ylab="PCA3", 
     main="PCA: Components 1 & 3", col=grpcol, pch=16)
legend("topright", legend=c("T", "N"), col=c("red", "blue"), pch=16)
```
Unsupervised learining methods:

K-means:
```{r}
set.seed(42)
k <- 2
kmeans_result <- kmeans(t(ex), k) #nstart=20) #try it
table(kmeans_result$cluster)
colnames(ex)
#plot(kmeans_result, data=t(ex)) + #geom_text(aes(label=gse$source_name_ch1),hjust=0,vjust=0)
plot.kmeans(kmeans_result, data = t(ex)) +
geom_text(aes(label=metadata$label),hjust=0,vjust=0)
```

Other method: the hierarchical one

```{r}
# Compute distance matrix between samples
dist_matrix <- dist(t(ex))
# Perform hierarchical clustering using Ward's method (minimizes within-cluster variance)
hc_result <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "ward.D")
# Define number of desired clusters
k <- 2
# Cut the dendrogram to form 'k' clusters
groups <- cutree(hc_result, k=k)
# Display how many samples fall into each cluster
table(groups)
# Plot the hierarchical clustering dendrogram
# 'hang = -1' makes leaf labels align at the same level
# Use source name metadata from the GEO dataset as labels
plot(hc_result, hang = -1, labels=metadata$label)
# Draw rectangles around the clusters in the dendrogram to visualize the 'k' groups
rect.hclust(hc_result, k = k, border = 2)  # red boxes around 2 clusters
#rect.hclust(hc_result, k = 4, which = NULL, x = NULL, h = NULL, border = 2, cluster = NULL) # red boxes to show groups
```
Random Forest: 

```{r}
library(randomForest)

# Use the existing label column ("T" for tumor, "N" for normal)
group <- as.factor(metadata$label)

# Log2-transform the expression matrix (samples are columns)
#small.eset <- log2(ex)  # ex: gene expression matrix, genes x samples
small.eset <- ex

# Ensure dimensions are as expected
dim(small.eset)  # Should be genes x samples (e.g., 20011 x 30)

# Train the Random Forest classifier (transpose so samples are rows)
set.seed(1234)
rf <- randomForest(x = t(small.eset), y = group, ntree = 1800)

# Plot the OOB error rate over number of trees
plot(rf, main = "Random Forest OOB Error Rate")

# Predict labels for the first 5 samples (just a test)
predict(rf, t(small.eset[, 1:5]))
# Plot sorted importance values
plot(sort(rf$importance, decreasing = TRUE), 
     main = "Sorted Feature Importance", 
     ylab = "Importance Score", xlab = "Rank")
varImpPlot(rf)  # if you want both MeanDecreaseAccuracy and Gini

# Get probe (gene) names
probe.names <- rownames(rf$importance)

# Top 200 by importance
top200 <- probe.names[order(rf$importance, decreasing = TRUE)[1:200]]

# Save to file
write.csv(top200, file = "probes-top200.txt", 
          quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE)
#exctract error rate 
# Get the final (i.e., last) OOB error rate
final_oob_error <- tail(rf$err.rate[, "OOB"], 1)
final_oob_accuracy <- 1 - final_oob_error

# Print
cat("Final OOB Error Rate:", round(final_oob_error * 100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Final OOB Accuracy:", round(final_oob_accuracy * 100, 2), "%\n")
```
Heatmap:
```{r}
#heatmap of the 25 top genes
library(RColorBrewer)     

top25 <- top200[1:25]

sig_genes_idx <- which(rownames(small.eset) %in% top25)
sig.eset <- small.eset[sig_genes_idx, ]

# Setup heatmap colors and annotation
hmcol <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "PuOr"))(256)
col_side_colors <- rep(hmcol[50], length(group))
col_side_colors[group == "T"] <- hmcol[200]

# Set sample labels as column names for heatmap
colnames(sig.eset) <- paste0(group, "_", 1:length(group))

heatmap(sig.eset, scale = "row", col = hmcol,
        ColSideColors = col_side_colors,
        main = "Heatmap of Top 25 Important Genes")

```
```{r}
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Subset top 25 important genes
top25 <- top200[1:25]
sig_genes_idx <- which(rownames(small.eset) %in% top25)
sig.eset <- small.eset[sig_genes_idx, ]

# Row-scale the data
scaled_eset <- t(scale(t(sig.eset)))

# Set column names to reflect group
colnames(scaled_eset) <- paste0(group, "_", 1:length(group))

# Create group annotation
annotation_col <- data.frame(Group = group)
rownames(annotation_col) <- colnames(scaled_eset)

# Set custom colors for T and N groups
ann_colors <- list(
  Group = c(T = "#F5E336",  # red for Tumor (CAF)
            N = "#9C6EC5")  # blue for Normal (NF)
)

# Define heatmap color palette
heat_colors <- colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")))(256)

# Plot heatmap with annotated colors
pheatmap(scaled_eset,
         color = heat_colors,
         annotation_col = annotation_col,
         annotation_colors = ann_colors,
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         show_rownames = TRUE,
         fontsize_row = 8,
         fontsize_col = 9,
         main = "Heatmap of Top 25 Important Genes")

```
LDA:
```{r}
library(genefilter)
labels <- factor(metadata$label, levels = c("N", "T"))
# Run row-wise t-tests to find DE genes
tt <- rowttests(ex, labels)

# Keep genes with p-value < 0.1
keepers <- which(tt$p.value < 0.1)
ex_filtered <- ex[keepers, ]

# Transpose matrix: samples x genes
tex <- t(ex_filtered)

# Combine expression with labels
dat <- cbind(as.data.frame(tex), label = labels)
# Get indices for normal and tumor samples
normal_idx <- which(dat$label == "N")
tumor_idx <- which(dat$label == "T")

# Randomly select 5 from each group for test set
set.seed(42)
train_normal <- sample(normal_idx, length(normal_idx) - 5)
train_tumor <- sample(tumor_idx, length(tumor_idx) - 5)
train <- c(train_normal, train_tumor)

# Remaining samples go to test set
test <- setdiff(1:nrow(dat), train)
# Fit LDA model
library("MASS")
mod <- lda(label ~ ., data = dat, prior = c(0.5, 0.5), subset = train)

# Predict on training set (for visualization)
mod.values <- predict(mod, dat[train, ])
#plot(mod.values$x[,1], ylab = "LDA Axis")
#text(mod.values$x[,1], labels = dat$label[train],col = as.numeric(dat$label[train]) + 1)

#plot:
library(ggplot2)

df_plot <- data.frame(
  sample = 1:length(train),
  lda1 = mod.values$x[,1],
  label = dat$label[train]
)

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = sample, y = lda1, color = label, label = label)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_text(vjust = -1, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(title = "LDA Axis 1 (Training Set)",
       x = "Sample Index", y = "LDA Axis 1") +
  theme_minimal()

# Predict on test set
preds <- predict(mod, dat[test, ])

# Show confusion matrix
table(Predicted = preds$class, Actual = dat$label[test])
# Convert labels to numeric: N = 1, T = 2
actual_numeric <- as.numeric(dat$label[test])
pred_numeric <- as.numeric(preds$class)

# Plot ROC curve
library("pROC")
roc_obj <- plot.roc(actual_numeric, pred_numeric,
                    col = "blue", print.auc = TRUE,
                    main = "LDA ROC Curve - GSE22862")
```

LASSO

```{r}
library(glmnet)
library(pROC)

# Transpose log2-transformed expression data (genes x samples → samples x genes)
dat <- t(ex)

# Create binary response: T = 1, N = 0
y <- ifelse(metadata$label == "T", 1, 0)

# Class-balanced train/test split
set.seed(123)
t_idx <- which(y == 1)
n_idx <- which(y == 0)

n_train_t <- round(0.7 * length(t_idx))
n_train_n <- round(0.7 * length(n_idx))

train_t <- sample(t_idx, n_train_t)
train_n <- sample(n_idx, n_train_n)
train_idx <- c(train_t, train_n)
test_idx <- setdiff(seq_along(y), train_idx)

# Fit LASSO logistic regression on training data
fit <- glmnet(dat[train_idx, ], y[train_idx], 
              family = "binomial", standardize = FALSE)

# Cross-validation to select lambda
cfit <- cv.glmnet(dat[train_idx, ], y[train_idx], 
                  family = "binomial", standardize = FALSE)

# Plot cross-validation curve
plot(cfit)

# Extract non-zero coefficients at optimal lambda
coef_best <- coef(cfit, s = "lambda.min")
print(coef_best)

# Predict probabilities on test data
pred_prob <- predict(cfit, dat[test_idx, ], s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
pred_class <- ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = y[test_idx])

# Plot ROC curve
roc_obj <- roc(y[test_idx], as.numeric(pred_prob))
plot(roc_obj, col = "darkred", print.auc = TRUE, main = "LASSO ROC Curve - GSE22862")

```

RF vs LDA vs LASSO
```{r}
library(genefilter)   # For row-wise t-tests
library(caret)        # For model training, cross-validation, and performance comparison
library(ggplot2)      # For plotting model comparison results
library(glmnet)       # For LASSO logistic regression (used internally by caret)
# Use your existing label column
group <- factor(metadata$label)  # factor with levels "N", "T"

tt <- rowttests(ex, group)
keepers <- which(tt$p.value < 0.1)  # raw p-value filter; or use adjusted p-values if you want #tt$p.adjust < 0.1
ex2 <- ex[keepers, ]  # subset genes by filter

# Prepare data for modeling: transpose to samples x genes
dat <- as.data.frame(t(ex2))
dat$Class <- group  # Add class labels

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(1234)

# Define train control for 5-fold CV
control <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)
metric <- "Accuracy"

# Train LDA model
fit.lda <- train(Class ~ ., data = dat, method = "lda", metric = metric, trControl = control)

# Train Random Forest model
fit.rf <- train(Class ~ ., data = dat, method = "rf", metric = metric, trControl = control)

# Train LASSO (glmnet) model
# Use alpha = 1 for LASSO (not ridge)
fit.lasso <- train(Class ~ ., data = dat, method = "glmnet",
                   family = "binomial",
                   tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 1, 
                                          lambda = seq(0.01, 1, by = 0.05)),
                   trControl = control,
                   metric = metric)  #10^seq(-4, 1, length.out = 100)

# Plot LASSO tuning results
plot(fit.lasso)

# Compare model performances
results <- resamples(list(LDA = fit.lda, RF = fit.rf, Lasso = fit.lasso))
summary(results)
ggplot(results) + labs(y = "Accuracy", title = "Model Comparison (5-fold CV)")

mean_accuracies_cv <- sapply(results$values[, grep("Accuracy", colnames(results$values))], mean)
print(mean_accuracies_cv)


# Repeat with repeated 5x5-fold CV for more stable estimates
control2 <- trainControl(method = "repeatedcv", number = 5, repeats = 5)

fit.lda.2 <- train(Class ~ ., data = dat, method = "lda", metric = metric, trControl = control2)
fit.rf.2 <- train(Class ~ ., data = dat, method = "rf", metric = metric, trControl = control2)
fit.lasso.2 <- train(Class ~ ., data = dat, method = "glmnet",
                     family = "binomial",
                     tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 1, 
                                            lambda = seq(0.01, 1, by = 0.05)), # should try this 10^(seq(-4,1,by=0.3 ))
                     trControl = control2,
                     metric = metric)

results2 <- resamples(list(LDA = fit.lda.2, RF = fit.rf.2, Lasso = fit.lasso.2))
summary(results2)
ggplot(results2) + labs(y = "Accuracy", title = "Model Comparison (Repeated 5x5 CV)")


mean_accuracies_rcv <- sapply(results2$values[, grep("Accuracy", colnames(results2$values))], mean)
print(mean_accuracies_rcv)
```
SCUDO pt.1:
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(caret)
library(rScudo)
library(igraph)

# Ensure expression matrix is log2-transformed
#small.eset <- log2(ex)  # ex = expression matrix: genes x samples

# Use metadata$label, mapping T/N to Tumor/Normal for SCUDO
group <- metadata$label  # Should contain "T" and "N"
f <- factor(group, levels = c("N", "T"), labels = c("Control", "Tumor"))

#Train/test split
set.seed(123)
inTrain <- createDataPartition(f, list = FALSE)
trainData <- small.eset[, inTrain]
testData <- small.eset[, -inTrain]

# Step 3: Train SCUDO signatures
trainRes <- scudoTrain(trainData, groups = f[inTrain],  
                       nTop = 25, nBottom = 25, alpha = 0.05)
print(trainRes)

# Inspect gene signatures
head(upSignatures(trainRes)[, 1:5])
head(consensusUpSignatures(trainRes))

# Step 4: Visualize training network
trainNet <- scudoNetwork(trainRes, N = 0.2)
scudoPlot(trainNet, vertex.label = NA)

# Step 5: Apply SCUDO to test set
testRes <- scudoTest(trainRes, testData, f[-inTrain], 
                     nTop = 25, nBottom = 25)
testNet <- scudoNetwork(testRes, N = 0.2)
scudoPlot(testNet, vertex.label = NA)

# Step 6: Clustering (optional)
if (!is.connected(testNet)) {
  components <- clusters(testNet)
  biggest_cluster <- which.max(components$csize)
  subgraph <- induced_subgraph(testNet, which(components$membership == biggest_cluster))
  testClust <- cluster_spinglass(subgraph, spins = 2)
  plot(testClust, subgraph, vertex.label = NA)
} else {
  testClust <- cluster_spinglass(testNet, spins = 2)
  plot(testClust, testNet, vertex.label = NA)
}

# Step 7: Classification
classRes <- scudoClassify(trainData, testData, N = 0.25,
                          nTop = 12, nBottom = 12,
                          trainGroups = f[inTrain], alpha = 0.5)

# Step 8: Evaluate classification
confMat <- caret::confusionMatrix(classRes$predicted, f[-inTrain])
print(confMat)
```

SCUDO pt.2:
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(caret)
library(rScudo)
library(igraph)

# Step 1: Prepare class labels
group <- metadata$label  # assuming it's a factor with "T" and "N"
f <- factor(group, levels = c("N", "T"), labels = c("Control", "Tumor"))

# Step 2: Partition the data into training and test sets
set.seed(123)
inTrain <- createDataPartition(f, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
trainData <- ex[, inTrain]
testData <- ex[, -inTrain]

# Step 3: Run SCUDO training on training set
trainRes <- scudoTrain(trainData, groups = f[inTrain],  
                       nTop = 25, nBottom = 25, alpha = 0.05)

# Step 4: Tune nTop & nBottom using caret (cross-validation)
set.seed(123)
model <- scudoModel(nTop = (2:6)*5, nBottom = (2:6)*5, N = 0.25)
control <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, 
                        summaryFunction = multiClassSummary,
                        classProbs = TRUE)

cvRes <- train(x = t(trainData), y = f[inTrain], 
               method = model, trControl = control)

# Step 5: Use best nTop/nBottom for SCUDO testing
bestTop <- cvRes$bestTune$nTop
bestBottom <- cvRes$bestTune$nBottom

testRes <- scudoTest(trainRes, testData, f[-inTrain], 
                     nTop = bestTop, nBottom = bestBottom)

# Step 6: Visualize test network
testNet <- scudoNetwork(testRes, N = 0.2)
scudoPlot(testNet, vertex.label = NA)

# Step 7: Classification using best parameters
classRes <- scudoClassify(trainData, testData, 
                          N = 0.25,
                          nTop = bestTop, nBottom = bestBottom,
                          trainGroups = f[inTrain], alpha = 0.05)

# Step 8: Performance Evaluation
confMat <- confusionMatrix(classRes$predicted, f[-inTrain])
print(confMat)

```

Traduction of affimetrix IDs in symbols:

```{r}
library(GEOquery)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
# Load platform annotation from GEO
gpl <- getGEO("GPL5175", AnnotGPL = TRUE)
gpl_table <- Table(gpl)
top_annot <- gpl_table[gpl_table$ID %in% top200, c("ID", "GB_LIST")]
# Get all unique RefSeq IDs from GB_LIST
refseq_ids <- unique(unlist(strsplit(top_annot$GB_LIST, ",")))

# Map RefSeq to gene symbols
refseq_to_symbol <- mapIds(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys = refseq_ids,
  column = "SYMBOL",
  keytype = "REFSEQ",
  multiVals = "first"
)
# Assign the first available gene symbol for each GB_LIST
top_annot$SYMBOL <- sapply(top_annot$GB_LIST, function(gb_list) {
  ids <- unlist(strsplit(gb_list, ","))
  matched <- refseq_to_symbol[ids]
  matched <- matched[!is.na(matched)]
  if (length(matched) > 0) matched[[1]] else NA
})
write.csv(top_annot, "top200_with_symbols.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
library(GEOquery)
library(limma)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(genefilter)

# Step 3: Subset tt to only top 200 probes
tt$ID <- rownames(tt)
tt_top200 <- tt[tt$ID %in% top200, c("ID", "dm", "p.value")]

# Step 4: Load GPL annotation for top probes
gpl <- getGEO("GPL5175", AnnotGPL = TRUE)
gpl_table <- Table(gpl)
top_annot <- gpl_table[gpl_table$ID %in% top200, c("ID", "GB_LIST")]

# Step 5: Map RefSeq -> Gene symbols
refseq_ids <- unique(unlist(strsplit(top_annot$GB_LIST, ",")))
refseq_to_symbol <- mapIds(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys = refseq_ids,
  column = "SYMBOL",
  keytype = "REFSEQ",
  multiVals = "first"
)

top_annot$SYMBOL <- sapply(top_annot$GB_LIST, function(gb_list) {
  ids <- unlist(strsplit(gb_list, ","))
  matched <- refseq_to_symbol[ids]
  matched <- matched[!is.na(matched)]
  if (length(matched) > 0) matched[[1]] else NA
})

# Step 6: Merge annotation with top 200 differential stats
merged <- merge(top_annot, tt_top200, by = "ID")
merged <- merged[!is.na(merged$SYMBOL), ]

# Step 7: Prepare pathfindR input
pathfinder_input <- merged[, c("SYMBOL", "dm", "p.value")]
colnames(pathfinder_input) <- c("Gene.symbol", "logFC", "p")

# Preview
head(pathfinder_input)
```
Functional enrichment analysis:
```{r}
library(gprofiler2)
# Remove NA values and duplicates
gene_list <- pathfinder_input$Gene.symbol
gostres <- gost(
  query = gene_list,
  organism = "hsapiens",
  ordered_query = FALSE,
  multi_query = FALSE,
  significant = TRUE,
  exclude_iea = FALSE,
  measure_underrepresentation = FALSE,
  evcodes = FALSE,
  user_threshold = 0.05,
  correction_method = "g_SCS",
  domain_scope = "annotated",
  custom_bg = NULL,
  numeric_ns = "",
  sources = NULL,
  as_short_link = FALSE
)
heaVd(gostres$result)
gostplot(gostres, capped = TRUE, interactive = TRUE)
p <- gostplot(gostres, capped = TRUE, interactive = FALSE)

# Optionally highlight specific terms (example terms below)
#publish_gostplot(p, 
# highlight_terms = c("GO:0048013", "REAC:R-HSA-3928663"), 
# filename = "gprofiler_plot.pdf"
#)
bp_terms <- subset(gostres$result, source == "GO:BP")
head(bp_terms[order(bp_terms$p_value), ])
# Identify columns that are lists
list_cols <- sapply(bp_terms, is.list)

# Collapse list columns into comma-separated strings
bp_terms[list_cols] <- lapply(bp_terms[list_cols], function(x) sapply(x, function(y) paste(y, collapse = ",")))

# Now write to CSV
write.csv(bp_terms, "GO_BP_enrichment_top200.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

Network analysis:
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(pathfindR)

# Run enrichment analysis with actual data
results_R <- run_pathfindR(
  input = pathfinder_input,
  gene_sets = "Reactome",   # You can also try "GO-BP", "KEGG"
  iterations = 1            # For exploratory analysis; use 10+ for final results
)

# View top enriched pathways
head(results_R)

# Visualize a term-gene network graph
term_gene_graph(results_R, num_terms = 10)

# Cluster enriched pathways
#clustered_results <- cluster_enriched_terms(results_R)

# View clustered results
#head(clustered_results)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(pathfindR)

# Run enrichment analysis with actual data
results_BP <- run_pathfindR(
  input = pathfinder_input,
  gene_sets = "GO-BP",   # You can also try "GO-BP", "KEGG"
  iterations = 1            # For exploratory analysis; use 10+ for final results
)

# View top enriched pathways
head(results_BP)

# Visualize a term-gene network graph
term_gene_graph(results_BP, num_terms = 10)

# Cluster enriched pathways
#clustered_results_GOBP <- cluster_enriched_terms(results_GOBP)

# View clustered results
#head(clustered_results_GOBP)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(pathfindR)

# Run enrichment analysis with actual data
results_CC <- run_pathfindR(
  input = pathfinder_input,
  gene_sets = "GO-CC",   # You can also try "GO-BP", "KEGG"
  iterations = 1            # For exploratory analysis; use 10+ for final results
)

# View top enriched pathways
head(results_CC)

# Visualize a term-gene network graph
term_gene_graph(results_CC, num_terms = 10)

# Cluster enriched pathways
#clustered_results_CC <- cluster_enriched_terms(results_CC)

# View clustered results
#head(clustered_results_CC)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(pathfindR)

# Run enrichment analysis with actual data
results_GOMF <- run_pathfindR(
  input = pathfinder_input,
  gene_sets = "GO-MF",   # You can also try "GO-BP", "KEGG"
  iterations = 1            # For exploratory analysis; use 10+ for final results
)

# View top enriched pathways
head(results_GOMF)

# Visualize a term-gene network graph
term_gene_graph(results_GOMF, num_terms = 10)

# Cluster enriched pathways
clustered_results_GOMF <- cluster_enriched_terms(results_GOMF)

# View clustered results
head(clustered_results_GOMF)
```
